title: tephrosia nubica subsp. arabica
intro: Tephrosia nubica subsp. arabica, also known as white sericite or Arabica spider-flower, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Tephrosia nubica subsp. arabica include reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and promoting wound healing, making it a valuable herbal remedy. Tephrosia nubica subsp. arabica can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional folk medicine to make poultices and salves.
